Courageous KidzA Safe Haven for Kids with Cancer
MissionProgramsImpactNewsVolunteer Give
Charleston, South Carolina · Since 2006

When a child fights cancer, the whole family needs a safe haven.

Courageous Kidz walks beside South Carolina families from diagnosis, through treatment and beyond, with gas cards, groceries, celebrations and a community that understands.

$50 a month keeps a family on the road to treatment at MUSC, up to three trips a week.

Give hope today

Processed securely through our existing donation system. Amount examples are illustrative.

Photo: Courageous Kidz families
Our mission

Year-round, unforgettable experiences for children with cancer and their families in South Carolina, always free of charge.

We provide much needed respite, mutual support and positive activities to these wonderful children and their families. Our goal is to relieve as much stress as possible, so the family can concentrate on helping the child with cancer get well again.

We are more than a support community. The kids and their families become part of our loving Courageous Kidz family, forever.

“The child has the disease, but the whole family has the cancer.”
Debby “Flash” Stephenson · Founder
Our founder

Four decades beside families in the fight

In 1982, Debby Stephenson originated Camp Happy Days as a Realtors' service project. Under her leadership it grew from 29 children and a $5,000 budget to serving more than 200 children and their families a year.

She stepped away in 2005 to care for her husband, Charlie, through his own battle with cancer. But the phone kept ringing: families needed her particular kind of help. In 2006 she founded Courageous Kidz to give it, personal, immediate and always free.

The kids call her “Flash.”

97.7%

Almost every dollar goes straight to the kids and their families.

We are small on purpose. No big offices, no big salaries. 97.7% of donated funds directly reach children with cancer and their families, as respite, aid and unforgettable experiences.

What your support did recently

Quiet help, delivered week after week.

44
times we stepped in with family aid last quarter: gas, food, utilities, lodging
37
kidz caught their first wave at our Shaka Surf beach day on Folly Beach
120+
children wake up to a full Christmas morning through donor kindness
Year-round programs

Whatever it takes to make life a little brighter

Every program exists for one reason: to lift the weight of childhood cancer off a family, even for a day.

Fun-Tastic Family Reunion

A day away from the disease: games, boat rides, burgers and music with families who understand.

A Mile of Smiles

Surprise celebrations, shopping trips and movie nights that lift a child's spirits mid-fight.

And through the year

  • Pinz-N-Pizza · family bowling nights
  • Cowabonga Beach Day · surf lessons
  • Moms Weekend Retreat · time to breathe
  • Healing Hearts · bereavement support

Give the Magic of Hope

Our logo was drawn by a young child fighting cancer. Asked how he stays so brave, he answered: “Because I'm courageous, like a lion.”
